I'm running this as an integration through HACS on HA OS.. .everything seems to be working but this log annoys me...
How do i fix it?

firstcommentafterclosing.body:
[EDIT - I'm an idiot - looks like I missed the option to set the Amazon region when configuring the component - so I'll delete and try again, actually READING the screen and not taking the default region. Sorry for being a dunce, I'm tired.]
Original post left as a warning for others....
"I apologise if I'm necroposting, but I've also run into this issue (in a fresh install of alexa_media_player 3.6.3) and my amazon.co.uk account shows my country/region set correctly to "United Kingdom" and I can see all my devices both in Amazon's "Devices" tab and also in the HA media_player list now, but I can't communicate with them, getting the same error as the OP above. In my logs I can see:
2021-02-01 00:00:33 DEBUG (MainThread) [alexapy.alexalogin] Login created for my_email - amazon.com
Does this mean that I somehow messed up the app setup on the Amazon site? The documentation links to the amazon.com domain to generate the 2FA key. but I thought I should log in to the UK site instead (https://www.amazon.co.uk/a/settings/approval), which is where I got my built-in 2FA key, which successfully worked when configuring the custom component? But that doesn't seem to have configured the media_player for my region.
I didn't see any instructions in the installation guide on how to set a non-US region, but if they exist please point me to them?"

lastcommentafterclosing.body:
Instead of starting from scratch..is there a way to edit a HA file to change the region too ?

It only takes about a minute for me to re  & re the integration. but if you prefer editing a system critical file, it's inside /config/.storage/core.config_entries.  Just don't mess it up or you'll end up spending far more time fixing the botch than doing a re & re...

lastcommentbeforeclosing.body:
If everything is working, then just ignore the warning. But you should make sure your Amazon account is registered to the domain you're using. If not, you can change it. And that devices are also registered in the right domain.
But again, if it works for you, I wouldn't change anything.

2172.bodyText:
Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.
I have noticed that the temperature value from the Amazon Thermostat updates infrequently in Home Assistant, i.e there is a delay in showing the actual temperature.
Describe the solution you'd like
Since I have created automations that work best if the temperature is updated frequently, it would be beneficial if the thermostat's refresh rate can be increased.  In the Alexa app I have noticed that the thermostat's temperature value does update frequently, so this should be possible.
Describe alternatives you've considered
I can create routines through the Alexa app based on the thermostat temperature, but the options there are less robust, so I would much prefer to create my automations in Home Assistant.

source.title: Amazon Thermostat reports incorrect temperature

firstcommentafterclosing.body: After #2177 is resolved, may this issue be reconsidered for a fix, please? This is an ongoing issue for myself and anyone else who uses the Amazon Thermostat with Home Assistant, if any automations are used that depend on an accurate thermostat temperature.

lastcommentbeforeclosing.body: No, you can trigger manual refreshes using HA already. We already have enough people getting 429 errors, #2177 so increasing load on Amazon will not be done officially.

firstcommentafterclosing.body:
I worked around this problem by using the alexa media player integration and using an automation to send a command to alexa.
Note that media_player.entry is the alexa where I send all my commands to just in case she ever decides to respond but the media_content_id I am specifying everything I want to do.

If I can code this so that the media content id is dynamic then I can create a dashboard with station buttons just using the voice commands.
I've used this method quite a few times particulary when I can't get devices working with home assistant but they work fine with alexa voice commands. You just have to remember to write "how it sounds"

Make sure 2FA OTP is enabled for your Amazon account.
Make sure you haven't previously checked the box Don't require OTP on this browser when you've been logging into https://amazon.com` (or whatever domain your account is in).  If you have then try clearing then via Require OTP on all devices in Amazon > Your account > Login and security > Two-Step Verification (2SV) Settings

2376.bodyText:
Describe the bug
I installed the Alexa Media Player integration and it picked up all my Alexa devices including some old unused ones -- all good.
It also seems to have created two sets of alexa entities for this_device and this_device2
I was told these represent Alexa on my phone and was thinking perhaps the duplication was due to my wife and I both having Alexa on our phones.
I am able to see alarms & timers on my regular Alexa devices -- as well as interact with them via Node Red.
However, I can't seem to interact with either of the this_device instances.

Expected behavior
I would expect this_device to wok similarly to my other physical Alexa echo and show devices.
But I can't seem to interact with the phone this_device versions.

Screenshots



Home-assistant (version): 2024.7.4


alexa_media (version from const.py or HA startup): 4.2.1


alexapy (version from pip show alexapy or HA startup):


Amazon 2FA is enabled (y/n) y <!---We will not debug login issues if unanswered--->:


Additional context
Not sure how to fix or debug this.

Am I write in believing that this_device refers to my phone not and the functionality of its entities should be similar if not the same as those of a physical echo dot?
Is there a way to get the Alexa integration to regenerate/refresh the connection to my phone(s)?
Should I delete the device this_device and this_device2 and all associated entities?

Any help and pointers here would be appreciated!
Thanks in advance!

firstcommentafterclosing.body:
Ahhh.
It seems that the "notify.alexa_media" service does the same thing regardless of which device I reference (whether one of the copies of this_device or a true echo device) -- it just beeps all my alexa devices and sends a toast message to my phone, regardless of the entity referenced.
If this is all you can do with "this_device", why create "this_device" in the first place? And in particular, why create multiple versions of it?
It seems to add absolutely nothing yet consumes state table space as the corresponding entities (5 for every version of this_device) continue to enter useless data.
Wouldn't it be better for the integration to filter out "this_device" and not create useless and confusing entities for it -- after all even the vanilla "this_deviceN" naming convention is totally non-obvious? (and I can't even figure which this_device is associated with which phone precisely because there is no unique associated functionality)
I could of course just disable the entities but would be nice if I could prevent their creation in the first place since by your report they truly add zero incremental value.

lastcommentafterclosing.body:
Wouldn't it be better for the integration to filter out "this_device" and not create useless and confusing entities for it

The integration simply processes and adds all echo devices in your Alexa account.  You have the option to exclude it or any other discovered device via exclude_devices (or Exclude these devices from all (comma separated)) in the integration's configuration.

lastcommentbeforeclosing.body:
this_device is the Alexa app on your phone.  If you have two phones with the app logged into the same Amazon account then you would see two this_device entities and HA renames the 2nd one with '2' so this_device2 is the second Alexa app instance on another phone.
The Alexa app does not have the same features as a physical Echo device as it is not even trying to emulate an echo device.  The app (and therefore its this_device entity) is simply a means to an end which is being able to interface with your account in the Amazon Alexa Cloud setup.  The one thing it does support is the following:
service: notify.alexa_media_this_device
data:
  title: Test
  message: This is a test
  data:
    type: push

The integration has no control over how the app is designed  or behaves.
The Alexa app simply does not allow for interfacing with your anything native on your phone like alarms and timers.

Additional context

I have found a partial workaround to add the amazon.co.uk config entry, but it does not result in a functioning AMP integration. Instead, the integration prompts to be reconfigured (possibly one of the other issues such as #2453), and has some strange behaviour possibly as a result of the same account being registered in two config entries (conflicting pickle file?)
The workaround to add the config entry:

Add the AMP integration but using the amazon.com region.
Once logged in to amazon, the AMP integration will be added, but will fail to start.

Click the 3 dot menu for the failing entry and select "System options"
Turn off both options and save:

Click "Add entry" and complete the setup this time for amazon.co.uk region

The integration should add successfully, but will then prompt to be re-configured:

Remove the amazon.com entry.

Edit: Removing the entry seemingly removes both the amazon.com and amazon.co.uk entries. Possibly a conflicting ID (email?) causing some strange behaviour.

source.title: Integration stop working after some time: `Login error detected; not contacting API`
Integration no more loaded after reinstallation: `KeyError: 'public_url'`
Do Mp3s still work through this integration?

For everyone's information, YAML configuration is being deprecated so you should start using UI config instead.
A new WARNING will soon be issued:

YAML configuration of Alexa Media Player is deprecated and will be removed in version 4.14.0. There will be no automatic import of this. Please remove it from your configuration, restart Home Assistant and use the UI to configure it instead. Settings > Devices and services > Integrations > ADD INTEGRATION

Also, do not use v4.13.8.  Use v4.13.7 until v4.13.9 is released.

lastcommentbeforeclosing.body:
I worked around the issue by providing a default value for account[CONF_PUBLIC_URL] on line 189. That allowed me configure and load the integration
                        CONF_PUBLIC_URL: account.get(CONF_PUBLIC_URL, 'https://my.domain/'),

THIS WORKED, THANKS @anthonyCarigny !!!!!!!!!!!

24h later.... It crashed again

When an Alexa device is set to Dutch language, you cannot control it or see any status updates. The devices get detected but every entity shows as unavailable
To Reproduce
Set a device to use the Dutch language and check after configuring the integration. English language devices are normally detected and are able to be operated, Dutch devices do show up, but all operations are not available
Expected behavior
To be able to see the status of and controll when the device is in Dutch language.
Screenshots

System details

Home Assistant version: 2024.8.2
alexa_media version (from const.py or HA startup log): 4.12.10
alexapy version (from pip show alexapy in homeasssistant container or HA startup log):
Is Amazon 2FA/2SV enabled <!---We will not debug login issues if unanswered---> (y/n): yes
Amazon Domain: amazon.nl

Debug Logs (alexa_media & alexapy)
Please provide logs.
Additional context
Dutch language support has just gone generally available, so maybe that is why it does not work yet.

File "/usr/local/lib/python3.12/http/cookies.py", line 312, in setitem
raise CookieError("Invalid attribute %r" % (K,))
http.cookies.CookieError: Invalid attribute 'partitioned'

@GJurriens You need to replace the python3.12/http/cookies.py with the one from Python 3.13 beta PR gh-112713 which will add support for the new Partitioned attribute in the Morsel object in http.cookies.

firstcommentafterclosing.body:
Hello!
I had this problem too, I managed to solve it by turning off the /config/custom_components/alexa_media directory
Then restart the HA, and install the Alexa Media Player integration in HACS again... and everything will be fine!

lastcommentafterclosing.body:
Hello!
I had this problem too, I managed to solve it by turning off the /config/custom_components/alexa_media directory
Then restart the HA, and install the Alexa Media Player integration in HACS again... and everything will be fine!

I've tried this method and it redirects me to 3 methods:


Im also having this issue, im new to HA and need this integration

This solution was found posted by another on a 3 day old post and I am pasting them here as it solved my issue.

Through trial and error trying to figure out the flow sequence, I was able to trick it and get to the OTP window.
After you've cycled through name, password and back to name, select the option Create Account New to Amazon?
Fill in a first & last name (anything at all as not going to matter so "Q W" is quick and easy to type!)
Amazon will then inform you that an account with your email already exists.
At that point re-select "Sign in Already a customer?" and proceed with the email and password windows and then the OTP window should appear. By that point though, the integration generated OTP has likely expired but you can just enter your current app generated OTP code and it should work.
If it returns to the integration add window and it just sits and spins and never progresses, that likely means the proxy listening session has expired and you'll have to repeat the process.

Does not work. I've tried both (a) internal IP (192.168.xx) and (b) nabu casa link. The integration just refuses to work.

2535.bodyText:
Describe the implementation
AMP 4.13.x will now fire an event when last_called is updated.
"alexa_media_last_called_event",
                    {
                        "last_called": self.device_serial_number,
                        "timestamp": self._last_called_timestamp,
                        "summary": self._last_called_summary,
                    },

This can be used in automations/scripts to wait for that event before continuing.  I've implemented a timeout in the wait_for_trigger of 30 seconds but that might be excessive.  You just want to avoid too short of a timeout in which the update last_called can succeed.  This wait_for_trigger avoids the necessity of delaying a fixed period of time before proceeding after manually calling alexa_media.update_last_called.  This method can then ensure that last_called was truly updated.  It can also be utilized to avoid the case where the integration is not working and your scripts/automations fail to execute as the Alexa service/entity does not exist by checking if the wait_for_trigger timed out and then skipping the relevant alexa service calls.
wait_for_trigger:
  - platform: event
    event_type: alexa_media_last_called_event
    event_data:
      summary: good night
timeout:
  hours: 0
  minutes: 0
  seconds: 30
  milliseconds: 0
continue_on_timeout: true

The event_data: summary: good night is the last thing in the history records that was said to Alexa and is included in the event so you can include that to ensure the last_called is truly correct for this script, or you can elect to not include that check and just proceed.
Following that, I have:
variables:
  alexa_last_called_updated: "{{ wait.remaining > 0 }}"

which is then used in if-then's to conditionally call actionable notifications or notify.alexa_media_last_called or whatever.
if:
  - alias: Test if alexa_last_called_updated renders true
    condition: template
    value_template: "{{ alexa_last_called_updated }}"
  - condition: state
    entity_id: input_boolean.coffee_prepared
    state: "off"
then:
  - data:
      text: Did you prepare your coffee?
      event_id: actionable_notification_coffee_prepared
      alexa_device: "{{ states('sensor.last_alexa') }}"
    action: script.activate_alexa_actionable_notification

or
if:
  - condition: template
    value_template: "{{ alexa_last_called_updated }}"
then:
  - data:
      message: Good morning
    action: script.notify_alexa_media

Another nifty thing I figured out was to create a new Home Assistant user that is only used by the Alexa Skill to integrate HA with Alexa. The Alexa skill then authenticates with HA using that username and you can use that to watch for state_changed events that were triggered by that user to automatically update last_called when Alexa has been asked to do something in HA like turn this or that on|off or when you say something that turns on an Alexa scene (which is an HA script which then waits for the new AMP event to fire).
alias: Alexa - Triggered by Smart Home Skill user account
description: >-
  Perform action `Alexa Media Player: Update Last Called Sensor` when entity
  states are changed by Alexa Smart Home Skill user account
trigger:
  - platform: event
    event_type: state_changed
    context:
      user_id: 0df463c1efc4490f900b09b143cae9c4
condition: []
action:
  - action: alexa_media.update_last_called
    data: {}
  - delay:
      hours: 0
      minutes: 0
      seconds: 6
      milliseconds: 0
trace:
  stored_traces: 30
mode: single
max_exceeded: silent

System details

Home Assistant version: 2024.9.2

alexapy version (from pip show alexapy in homeasssistant container or HA startup log): 1.29.2

Amazon Domain: amazon.ca

lastcommentbeforeclosing.body:
1. include_devices ONLY loads devices that have been listed and functions like a deny list.
2. Once you have set the Include/Exclude fields, clearing them out does not stick.
The second issue appears to be a legitimate bug, where once there is text in either the include or exclude fields, you can't clear it out through the UI. Deleting everything and clicking Submit appears to work but if you open Configure again you'll see the values are still there. The workaround I used is to edit the config/.storage/core.config_entries file directly. Just search for "alexa" to find the right line, then leave only an empty string ("") for include_devices and/or exclude_devices.

It's not a bug but a limitation of HA config flow.  Those fields are type 'str' and the only way to "empty" them seems to be by submitting a . I've added debug lines to the OptionsFlowHandler in config_flow.py and after erasing the field and clicking submit, the previous entry remains.  If I enter  in the field then it sets it to ' '.
My first try by simply deleting the contents from the field and clicking submit:

2577.bodyText:
Just wanted to share... I've been having login issues and when I tried to re-add my account it would only say -error- after properly signing in
Anyways, pihole was dns blocking api.amazonalexa.com:443
Added it to my white-list and was able to properly login with this app again
Thanks again for all the hard work dev
<3

lastcommentbeforeclosing.body: The problem has not yet been completely resolved, but some update (I don't know if it was from HA or the integration) has reduced the recurrence considerably. The errors now only appear in the event of an internet connection failure. In other words, the integration has no "tolerance" for it to understand the loss of connection as something that can happen. In fact, this is something that needs to be improved in HA as a whole.

2633.bodyText:
NOTE: For a feature to be created it has to be doable in the Amazon app because we are basically replicating the app for HA. If you can't find it in the app, we probably can't create it here.
Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.
I'm always frustrated when using Home Assistant Alerts with the alexa_media_player integration because the done_message (e.g., clear_notification) is forwarded and played as a voice notification via TTS on Alexa devices.
This issue typically occurs when using a Notify Group that contains both mobile phones and Alexa devices. The clear_notification is useful for clearing notifications on mobile phones, but it is unnecessary and disruptive when Alexa announces "clear_notification" via TTS.
Describe the solution you'd like
I would like an rule where specific messages are not forwarded to TTS on Alexa devices. Specifically, the clear_notification should:

Be processed by devices like Android phones to clear the notification.
Be ignored by Alexa media players to prevent it from being played via TTS.

Describe alternatives you've considered
As a workaround, I’ve considered using conditions in Home Assistant to manually avoid sending the clear_notification to Alexa devices. However, this solution is cumbersome and requires additional automations, making the system less flexible and harder to manage.
Additional context
In the current setup, every message sent via alert to Alexa devices is played via TTS. This feature would help streamline and separate how alerts are handled between different types of devices, specifically mobile phones vs. media players.
Thank you for considering this feature request!
Best regards,
Marco

Additional context
I don't know when this started, but AMP was previously working just fine and one day I noticed it stopped (my devices were unavailable). I deleted and attempted to reinstall the integration and this started. I've tried removing all traces of AMP including deleting from HACS and redownloading (with several reboots in between), but the error is the same. So either there is some kind of critical bug in AMP setup (unlikely since this has been going on for weeks and no one else has reported it) or there is some leftover file in my installation that I don't know about. The "bad marshal data" seems like a generic python issue that is usually resolved by clearing out __pycache__, but since I've tried deleting AMP and redownloading it I'm not really sure where else it could be storing these binaries.
The UI error is the same as #2293, but the cause looks different so I'm creating a new issue.

Describe the bug
The Alexa Media Player integration is experiencing issues with region mismatch and API errors. When using amazon.de, no devices are found, and API errors occur. Switching to amazon.com lists devices but they cannot be controlled.
To Reproduce
The integration fails to authenticate properly with amazon.de, resulting in no devices being listed and API errors. When switching to amazon.com, devices appear but cannot be controlled, with logs showing domain mismatch errors between amazon.com and amazon.de.

Configure Alexa Media Player with amazon.de
Observe that no devices are found and API errors occur.
Switch to amazon.com.
Devices are found but cannot be controlled; logs show domain mismatch errors.

Expected behavior
Devices should be discoverable and controllable using my Amazon region (amazon.de), without domain mismatch or authentication issues.
Screenshots
Login:

Errors after login (amazon.de)



Errors after login (amazon.de) with Set async_timeout to 300


Error after login (amazon.com)

Result of login in amazon.de and amazon.com

System details
Home Assistant version: Core 2024.11.3 - Supervisor 2024.11.4 - Operating System 13.2 - Frontend 20241106.2
alexa_media version: 4.13.7, 4.13.8 and 5.0.0 tested
alexapy version: 1.29.4
Is Amazon [2FA/2SV enabled]: Yes
Amazon Domain: amazon.de
Debug Logs (alexa_media & alexapy)
home-assistant_2024-11-28T22-18-34.509Z.log
Additional context
What I already tried:

Tried different versions of alexa_media
Installed alexa_media on another HA system (version 2024.9.1) with the same error.
Accessing https://alexa.amazon.com/api/devices-v2/device and https://alexa.amazon.de/api/devices-v2/device in a browser shows Alexa devices for both.
OpenHAB with Echo Control Binding (similar to Alexa Media Player) works without API issues; login and devices function correctly.
Disabled all Echo devices, Alexa apps, integrations/bindings for at least 12 hours due to "Too Many Requests" error, but same even after 12 hours
Tried various login options over three days.
Set async_timeout to 300; then encountered "Too Many Requests" error.
Changed code in manifest.json "requirements": ["alexapy>=1.28.2", "packaging>=20.3", "wrapt>=1.14.0"],
Tried putting the local HA-URL in the public URL
Include only one Alexa device in the config
Tried disabling the 2FA
Deleting all logged in devices in the the Alexa app

All of this did not work
It worked to integrate my wife's account (Amazon.de without 2FA). The devices appear there
Assumption:
The problem might accur due to the number of devices in my account (Total about 110 devices - Echos and some sensors via the xiaomi skill). The devices cannot be deleted from the alexa app - > unknown error

fix: home-assistant-matter-hub duplicated entities
feat: Detect HA matter bridge association

firstcommentafterclosing.body:
It is unfortunately not correct for a matter bridge to report the bridge's vendor on bridged devices. The vendor information there is meant to be that of the bridged device, e.g. "Philips" for a Hue bulb, not that of the bridge. The bridge vendor is only meant to be set on its own device node.
@danielbrunt57's idea of  getting the ID of the bridge device (which would still have the t0bst4r vendor, and also detail that it is a matter hub if one wanted to filter those out more generically) and checking an appliance ID prefix seems like a more viable long-term approach on first glance...

lastcommentafterclosing.body:
It is unfortunately not correct for a matter bridge to report the bridge's vendor on bridged devices. The vendor information there is meant to be that of the bridged device, e.g. "Philips" for a Hue bulb, not that of the bridge. The bridge vendor is only meant to be set on its own device node.
@danielbrunt57's idea of  getting the ID of the bridge device (which would still have the t0bst4r vendor, and also detail that it is a matter hub if one wanted to filter those out more generically) and checking an appliance ID prefix seems like a more viable long-term approach on first glance...

lastcommentbeforeclosing.body:
Yes, I forgot to set the vendor details for every device. Instead I only set it for the bridge. I’ve changed that and will release it tomorrow.

Thanks.  I've revised my PR from "TestVendor" to "tobst4r".

lastcommentafterclosing.body:
Same here, i am doing it as new install, it redirects me to login, but after logging in i get this.

Update. After following the 2fa Instructions. It worked.

Instead of using a separate 2FA code, you can also register a 2FA app with Alexa Media Player.

    Go to [Amazon.com's 2FA page](https://www.amazon.com/a/settings/approval) and Add new App. You should save this key so you can reuse it if you reinstall.
    Instead of scanning the QR code, select Can't scan the barcode.
    Select the bolded value under Enter your Key (e.g., 35T5 LQSY I5IO 3EFQ LGAJ I6YB JWBY JJPR PYT7 XPPW IDAK SQBJ CVXA)(Remove spaces)
    Enter the value from Step 3 in the Built-in 2FA App Key when adding the integration. 2FA should be automatically generated from now on and can be left blank.
    (Optional) All these steps only add another authentication method but do not activate 2FA. In case you haven’t enabled 2FA yet, follow Amazon's [instructions](https://www.amazon.com/gp/help/customer/display.html?nodeId=202073820).

The above seems to send music requests to async_play_tts_cloud_say which at the bottom sends out the error message mentioned in this issue. Not sure why the actual play_music call is only the default action and not used when the media_type is music.

The first blob is simply a way to gracefully handle the error that arises when the media type is music instead of leaving the users clueless without a response at all. "Sorry folks! Amazon doesn't allow streaming music like this." would be a more appropriate TTS response for this I think.
Regarding the second blob, I think Amazon previously allowed this functionality and Alexa Media Player was able to use it (I started using Home Assistant only about a year ago so not sure) but the support no longer exists and this is just remnants of the old code that allowed it. It could also be some placeholder code they could use if Amazon allows/re-allows this functionality in the future.
In fact, I have created a small PR for this (#2736) so that users get hear a more accurate error message while trying to play music using this integration instead of something vague.
EDIT: I checked out the code for play_music function (https://gitlab.com/keatontaylor/alexapy/-/blob/dev/alexapy/alexaapi.py?ref_type=heads#L728) in alexaapi file in alexapy from where this function is actually imported. Seems like the function is simply meant to do something like "Alexa, play   on ". So there doesn't seem to be anything that can actually control the playback or cast local music to the Alexa device since Amazon's 'walled garden' doesn't have any support for casting local music at all.

source.title: fix: Clear error message for music streaming
fix: clarify error message for music streaming (#2736)

lastcommentbeforeclosing.body:
That play_music function in gitlab indicates you can pass a provider and search string, which should work: https://gitlab.com/keatontaylor/alexapy/-/blob/dev/alexapy/alexaapi.py?ref_type=heads#L740-L741
Granted we'd need to pass some sort of valid provider, which would not likely work for local media but could be used for several services such as:

Amazon Music
Apple Music
iHeartRadio
Pandora
TuneIn


Thanks for explaining it in a better manner. You're right, it simply executes a search query which could be done with an automation like the one I am using (#2208 (comment)).
Maybe the Music Assistant team could implement a way to pass a search query with 'custom' content type to the media player device created by AMP to create an illusion of the music being cast on the Alexa media device but even in that case, Alexa device would only be able to play the track if it is available on one of its supported services.
Therefore, this method still won't allow the media_player entity created by this Integration to be used like a proper media player using Music Assistant since this approach will limit the ability to control playback fully or cast local media.

2734.bodyText:
Hello,
I think the alexa media player integration is great. I often use my echos for TTS announcements, e.g. the doorbell rang, washing machine ready.
However, my echos are often set to volume 1.
It would therefore be great if there was a parameter that could be added to a TTS announcement that raises the volume to a defined value, executes the TTS announcement and then resets the volume to the old value.
With Sonos integration, this is possible via the “announce” parameter.
Translated with DeepL.com (free version)

NOTE: For a feature to be created it has to be doable in the Amazon app because we are basically replicating the app for HA. If you can't find it in the app, we probably can't create it here.

no
Describe the solution you'd like
I often use my echos for TTS announcements, e.g. the doorbell rang, washing machine ready.
However, my echos are often set to volume 1.
It would therefore be great if there was a parameter that could be added to a TTS announcement that raises the volume to a defined value, executes the TTS announcement and then resets the volume to the old value.
With Sonos integration, this is possible via the “announce” parameter.

i dont know any alternative

firstcommentafterclosing.body: @lpercetti how'd you resolve the issue? It seems that many users are having problems with this. Any additional information would be extremely appreciated.

lastcommentafterclosing.body:
Hello @altjx,
I closed the issue because it's not strictly related to this integration, but more generally to Home Assistant Docker edition.
The solution I used is to overload the official homeassistant docker image using a Dockerfile that is preinstalling the python module.
Regards,

Ah ok. Thanks for the input!
EDIT: FWIW - I just tried it again and everything worked flawlessly. There were some home assistant updates over the last few weeks that I applied, so I assume this has something to do with it.

2751.bodyText:
IMPORTANT: Please search the issues, including closed issues, and the FAQ before opening a new issue. The template is mandatory; failure to use it will result in issue closure.
Describe the bug
If you enter any devices in the "Included devices" field of the Reconfiguration dialog and save you cannot then re-edit "included devices" to be blank. An empty string is not saved. A space is saved but still means that only included devices will be included.
To Reproduce

Click CONFIGURE and enter a device in the "included devices" field and save
Click CONFIGURE again and delete the device you entered leaving an empty string and save
Click CONFIGURE again and you will see the device hasn't been removed

Expected behavior
I would expect the device entered to be removed when I delete the text leaving an empty string and save,
System details


Home Assistant version:
Core 2024.12.3
Supervisor 2024.11.4
Operating System 14.0
Frontend 20241127.8


alexa_media version (from const.py or HA startup log): 5.0.4

source.title: Included Device Not Working
fix: Remove leading/trailing spaces from include_devices, exclude_devices

lastcommentbeforeclosing.body:
My question to anyone using "Include devices" is Why are you using that field?
For me it was a misunderstanding of how it worked. I thought I needed to explicitly add new devices to the list. When I realized that wasn't how it worked, that's when I ran into problems trying to remove it.

Having said that, I have disabled a lot of entities that it is creating because unfortunately all the HA entities that the Alexa Integration has created in Alexa get recreated again!
So I probably do need to tidy this up by using Include or Exclude. And in fact Include would probably be better as there a lot to be excluded.

2753.bodyText:
Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.
I'm trying to use custom command to tell Alexa to use a skill to unlock a door. The skill requires that my voice is recognized for that command. When I try with Alexa media player, the target Alexa device reports "I could not recognize your voice".
Describe the solution you'd like
A way to pass a voice ID with the custom command. Or a way to have Alexa "learn" the voice used by Alexa media player.

firstcommentafterclosing.body: Custom command utilizes the Amazon Simon Says skill and doesn't verbally  "speak" anything for an echo device to listen to and identify therefore this request is an impossibility.  Your lock skill has to hear a real voice speaking.

lastcommentafterclosing.body: Custom command utilizes the Amazon Simon Says skill and doesn't verbally  "speak" anything for an echo device to listen to and identify therefore this request is an impossibility.  Your lock skill has to hear a real voice speaking.

lastcommentbeforeclosing.body: The issue has received no activity for 60 days and will be closed in a week.

2756.bodyText:
IMPORTANT: Please search the issues, including closed issues, and the FAQ before opening a new issue. The template is mandatory; failure to use it will result in issue closure.
Describe the bug
After upgrading the alexa_media_player HACS integration to version 5.1.0 most of the entities from my Amazon Air Quality Monitors disappeared. Only the temperature entity still is beeing published by the integration. Reloading the integration does not help.
To Reproduce
Upgrade to version 5.1.0 and restart HA afterwards. Even after reloading the integration after these steps, only the "First Air Quality Monitor Temperature" entity for each Amazon Air Quality Monitor is available.
Expected behavior
With Version 5.0.5 there are 6 entities published by the integration for each Amazon Air Quality Monitor:

First Air Quality Monitor Carbon Monoxide
First Air Quality Monitor Humidity
First Air Quality Monitor Indoor Air Quality
First Air Quality Monitor Particulate Matter
First Air Quality Monitor Temperature
First Air Quality Monitor Volatile Organic Compounds

Screenshots

System details

Home Assistant version: 2024.12.3
alexa_media version (from const.py or HA startup log): 5.1.0
alexapy version (from pip show alexapy in homeasssistant container or HA startup log): 1.29.5
Is Amazon 2FA/2SV enabled <!---We will not debug login issues if unanswered---> (y/n): y
Amazon Domain: amazon.de

Debug Logs (alexa_media & alexapy)
Please provide logs.
Too much sensitive data to remove from the log. If there are specific entries you need, let me know.
Additional context

Good day all,
From my side i've the latest version up to date.
As last try i've deleted the integration entry and replay the configuration, selected the great domain (amazon.fr) done all the configuration without issue, all entities came back on this new configuration, the AQI entries are here to, but does not update, it stay as "unknow" the data does not come.

lastcommentbeforeclosing.body: Normally you'd just wait for that fix (which is a pull request) to be merged and released in a new version, which this one has. But if you see a PR and want to apply it before it it's released, I'd be happy to pass along my knowledge. I'm on my phone right now or I'd be more specific but after clicking the link to the PR, you want to look at "files changed" which is like tab 3 or 4 and it shows the original code on the left, new fcode on the right with minuses and plusses as to what was changed for each file. Above you can can see pull requests. Open have not been merged yet. Closed have been merged but may not be released as a new version yet, and new versions are packaged up as a zip file for HACS to process, which shows as current vrelease on the right side of the main code tab for this repository. I apologize in advance if my memory of it all is inaccurate or incorrect!

Recent versions have broken previously working doorbell notifications. I have tried recreating the automation action, but no sound is played from any device.
To Reproduce
Have an automation that includes a notification:

alias: Doorbell announcement
description: ""
triggers:

type: occupied
device_id: b8bbf005cb31eccca417c1cb2e4fb674
entity_id: 8539fc0da13e64704c1f7a6055f0aa19
domain: binary_sensor
trigger: device
conditions: []
actions:
action: notify.alexa_media_everywhere
metadata: {}
data:
message: There's somebody at the door
mode: single

Expected behavior
The message should play through all Echos

Home Assistant version: 2024.12.5
alexa_media version: 5.1.0
alexapy version : 1.29.5

2779.bodyText:
Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.
Currently, when using Alexa Media Player for TTS (Text-to-Speech) announcements, the only way to adjust the volume is by changing the overall device volume. However, this approach has several drawbacks:

Alexa emits a confirmation sound whenever the volume is adjusted, which can be disruptive.
The volume change is persistent, meaning it affects other activities like music playback or alarms.
There is no way to set a temporary TTS-specific volume that applies only to announcements.

This limitation makes it difficult to manage TTS messages without impacting the user experience for other Alexa functions.
Describe the solution you'd like
I propose adding an option to set a temporary TTS-specific volume that applies only during the announcement and does not affect the device's general volume settings. This could work as:

A parameter in the notify.alexa_media service (e.g., volume_tts) that sets a temporary volume for TTS.
An internal mechanism that adjusts the volume for TTS playback and restores it immediately afterward without triggering Alexa's confirmation sound.

This feature would allow users to send louder or quieter TTS messages without altering the device's default playback volume.
Describe alternatives you've considered

Using SSML tags like <prosody volume="x-loud"> within TTS messages. However, this approach is inconsistent and not supported in all scenarios.
Manually adjusting the volume before and after each TTS message using automations or scripts. This method works but causes Alexa's confirmation sound and requires additional delays to ensure proper execution.

Additional context
This feature is available in OpenHAB's Echo Control Binding, where users can define a separate TTS volume that does not affect the device's actual playback settings. Implementing a similar feature in Alexa Media Player would greatly enhance its usability for Home Assistant users.

2801.bodyText:
IMPORTANT: Please search the issues, including closed issues, and the FAQ before opening a new issue. The template is mandatory; failure to use it will result in issue closure. ✅
According to the wiki on tts, I should be able to target rooms, serialnumbers (for echo device for example) and other groupings, but it does not seem to work, except if the target is the alexa_media entity itself.
I crated a script to facilitate TTS action via a button in the dashboard and this is how it looks:

sequence:
  - action: notify.alexa_media
    data_template:
      title: Alexa Speak...
      data:
        type: tts
      message: "{{message}}"
      target: |
        [
          {%- for key, values in targets.items() -%}
            {%- if values is string -%}
              "{{ values }}"
            {%- else -%}
              {%- for value in values -%}
                "{{ value }}"{% if not loop.last %}, {% endif %}
              {%- endfor -%}
            {%- endif %}
          {%- if not loop.last %}, {% endif %}
          {%- endfor -%}
        ]
alias: Echo talk
description: ""
fields:
  message:
    selector:
      text: null
    name: Message
    required: true
  targets:
    selector:
      target:
        entity:
          domain: media_player
          integration: alexa_media
    name: Targets
    description: Select Amazon Echo devices or areas to send your message to
    required: true
    default:
      entity_id: media_player.echo_main
mode: queued
max: 15
It was already tricky to get this to work at all, because the direct output of a target selector has an extra level in the result. Example:

params:
  domain: notify
  service: alexa_media
  service_data:
    title: Alexa Speak...
    data:
      type: tts
    message: test
    target:
      area_id: office
  target: {}
Ended up with that ugly templating, difficult to figure out, because the result of the selector has a different type if it is a single item (string), or multiple (dictionary).
Having the right template in place, I was able to have it output the way the integration expects it (or at least matches the format in the example):
params:
  domain: notify
  service: alexa_media
  service_data:
    title: Alexa Speak...
    data:
      type: tts
    message: test
    target:
      - office
But that doesn't work. My assumption is that it would do an expansion, similar to what the UI does.
If I do expand in the UI (have to expand twice, as the echo device directly also doesn't work. room -> device -> entity), then it works:

params:
  domain: notify
  service: alexa_media
  service_data:
    title: Alexa Speak...
    data:
      type: tts
    message: test
    target:
      - media_player.echo_office_1
      - media_player.office
Is there something I am missing here?
Either way, thanks for the fine work on this integration. Have been looking into this a long time for a project, integrating directly to it, before I decided to use HA, which brought me here.
To Reproduce


HA Side Menu -> Settings -> Automations and Scenes -> Scripts
Click on 'Create Script'
Click the '...' in the top right corner -> Edit as YAML
Paste the yaml for the script:

sequence:
  - action: notify.alexa_media
    data_template:
      title: Alexa Speak...
      data:
        type: tts
      message: "{{message}}"
      target: |
        [
          {%- for key, values in targets.items() -%}
            {%- if values is string -%}
              "{{ values }}"
            {%- else -%}
              {%- for value in values -%}
                "{{ value }}"{% if not loop.last %}, {% endif %}
              {%- endfor -%}
            {%- endif %}
          {%- if not loop.last %}, {% endif %}
          {%- endfor -%}
        ]
alias: Echo talk
description: ""
fields:
  message:
    selector:
      text: null
    name: Message
    required: true
  targets:
    selector:
      target:
        entity:
          domain: media_player
          integration: alexa_media
    name: Targets
    description: Select Amazon Echo devices or areas to send your message to
    required: true
    default:
      entity_id: media_player.echo_main
mode: queued
max: 15

Save (give it a couple of seconds, otherwise it won't launch). You may see an error about the description, but that doesn't matter.
Again click on the ellipses of top right corner -> Run Script
You should be greeted with a popup similar to my screenshot above.
Type in any message and run some tests with indirect objects (like room or devices) as targets,
I expect you won't hear anything from Alexa (the bug I refer to). Head back to the script editor and click on TRACES to validate that the parameters are passed in correctly.

Expected behavior
I expected that my message would have been spoken by all echo devices within the specified areas.
Screenshots
Included in the description

firstcommentafterclosing.body:
Nice that @aman-sandhu joined the club, but that was not really what the issue was about. The issue I reported revolves around the notification component and its ability to target HA groups:

"target:" - The target Alexa devices. This can be the Friendly Name, Serial Number, entity_id, or Home Assistant Group.

As I previously mentioned, the idea of HA group can be quite ambiguous, as you can have groupings of any type. Given that expand is an actual function in HA (and can work with traditional groups, areas or entities), it is easy to assume the component takes advantage of this to be able to announce to any given scope.
To close this issue, I would say that either a patch that can resolve any type of group or a clarification in the wiki to remove the ambiguity. I haven't worked on it again yet, but it shouldn't be too difficult to make a template loop that could also be added to the wiki as a workaround to be able to target groups ubiquitously.

lastcommentafterclosing.body: Anyone can edit the Wiki!  Use your best English grammar, and avoid typos!!

2805.bodyText:
Current Behavior
When the DataUpdateCoordinator returns stale data (logging "Coordinator data for [entity_id] is too old to be returned"), the component attempts to enforce its last known state by sending commands to the device approximately every 90 seconds.
This results in:

Repeated set_light_state commands being sent to Alexa
Lights being turned back on after manual off commands directly to Alexa
Unnecessary API calls to Amazon's servers

In my case, the entity is a light that only connects to Alexa. I turn the light manually and a minute later, it pops back on.
Example log pattern:
DEBUG [custom_components.alexa_media.alexa_entity] Coordinator data for [entity_id] is too old to be returned
DEBUG [alexapy.alexaapi] set_light_state response: {'controlResponses': [{'code': 'SUCCESS' ...}]} for data: {"controlRequests": [{"entityId": "[entity_id]", "parameters": {"action": "turnOn"}}, {"entityId": "[entity_id]", "parameters": {"action": "setColorTemperature", "colorTemperatureName": "warm_white"}}]}

Desired Behavior
Add a configuration option to control how the component handles stale coordinator data. For example:
alexa_media:
      stale_data_behavior: "unknown"  # Options: "enforce" (current behavior) or "unknown"
When set to "unknown", instead of trying to enforce the last known state, the component would simply mark the entity state as unknown until fresh data is available.
Use Case
This would be particularly useful for:

Users who control devices through multiple interfaces (Alexa app, voice commands, etc.)
Reducing unnecessary API calls
Preventing devices from being turned back on when manually turned off

Current Workaround
Currently using a helper boolean that Alexa updates directly and a separate automation to maintain state, but this shouldn't be necessary.

firstcommentafterclosing.body: The pyotp package is not installed properly in HA.

lastcommentafterclosing.body:
When Alexa Media player is added via HACS and HA is restarted, HA should install the alexapy dependency and the alexapy dependency should install pyotp.  If you remove Alexa Media in HACS, restart, re-add Alexa Media in HACS and restart, that should install all the dependencies.
Or you can try pip install pyotp in the HA container - which is not just ssh.  ssh is a protected environment.  From there you would need to sudo docker exec -it homeassistant bash to get to the main HA container where you would then do pip install pyotp...

Describe the bug
I have the Alexa Media Player integration and use it in conjunction with an Alexa Echo Dot for an automation that is triggered by a motion detector.
So far everything works, but regularly (at least once a day, if not more often) neither the command “media_player.play_media” nor “media_player.media_pause” is executed, the media_pause feels even more often. The whole thing hasn't always worked perfectly, so it is independent of a particular software update of the integration, at least not noticeably.
As far as I can see, the command is also executed by the Home Assistant, at least no error message or anything like that appears, but if I execute “media_player.media_pause” while music is playing, for example, nothing happens. If I restart Home Assistant or click on the three dots under Integrations - Alexa Media Player and then on “Reload” and execute “media_player.media_pause” again, then it works immediately and I would say 100%. Until it doesn't work again during the course of the day or the next day at the latest. But to be honest, the integration for automations is hardly usable this way.
What could be the reason for this or what can I test?
Just in case it's relevant: I use 2FA for my Amazon account.
Thank you already!

Logger: alexapy.helpers
Source: custom_components/alexa_media/__init__.py:798
integration: Alexa Media Player (documentation, issues)
First occurred: 8:42:12 AM (1 occurrences)
Last logged: 8:42:12 AM

alexaapi.get_customer_history_records((<alexapy.alexalogin.AlexaLogin object at 0x7f8c856e38c0>,), {'max_record_size': 10}): Timeout error occurred accessing AlexaAPI: An exception of type CancelledError occurred. Arguments: ()

This timeout is a normal occurrence in alexapy due to the extremely restrictive nature of Amazon permitting access to customer_history_records and is only used to determine the last called alexa device and has no impact on anything else.  This has been plaguing everyone for 6-8 months now.

firstcommentafterclosing.body: Don't understand.  I've been using OTP successfully with Google Authenticator to log into Amazon for years.  You get a 6-digit code from Google Authenticator. lastcommentafterclosing.body:
@kadeschs I was also confused the same way you are. It is not looking for a code from your existing authenticator app. It is wanting you to set up the Media Player integration as an authenticator app itself. Once setup, you will have 2 apps that generates authentication codes (Google Authenticator on your phone, and Alexa Media Player for issuing its own codes internally).
Here are the instructions to setup a secondary authenticator app (Alexa Media Player):
https://github.com/alandtse/alexa_media_player/wiki/Configuration#2sv-authenticator-app-key---required
https://www.amazon.com/a/settings/approval

2852.bodyText:
Hi there,
 Any idea what this error is all about and how to fix it?

This error originated from a custom integration.

Logger: alexapy.helpers
Source: custom_components/alexa_media/__init__.py:798
integration: Alexa Media Player (documentation, issues)
First occurred: March 5, 2025 at 15:50:46 (18 occurrences)
Last logged: 02:20:17

alexaapi.get_customer_history_records((<alexapy.alexalogin.AlexaLogin object at 0x7f7215eb1a90>,), {'max_record_size': 10}): Timeout error occurred accessing AlexaAPI: An exception of type CancelledError occurred. Arguments: ()

firstcommentafterclosing.body: It's Amazon imposing limits on polling and it can't be fixed.  It's only used to update the last_called attributes of the echo devices.

lastcommentafterclosing.body: Oh ok.  Thank you so much for looking and letting me know.

lastcommentbeforeclosing.body: @R4ZZB3RRY this issue was automatically closed because it did not follow the issue template and this warning: IMPORTANT: Please search the issues, including closed issues, and the FAQ before opening a new issue. The template is mandatory; failure to use it will result in issue closure.

2855.bodyText:
IMPORTANT: Please search the issues, including closed issues, and the FAQ before opening a new issue. The template is mandatory; failure to use it will result in issue closure.
Describe the bug
I believe I have the same problem described in issue #2292, but as none of the answers to that issue fixed my problem I'm asking for help.
I configured Alexa Media Player as my OTP generator to my Amazon account's 2FA. By doing so, I blocked myself from logging into the account, as I can't generate or get any code to log in.

lastcommentbeforeclosing.body:
You can use the same 52-character key for both AMP and your own 2FA TOTP generator app (I use Bitwarden but there are many).or, can add more than one Authenticator app to Amazon but since you've effectively locked yourself out of your account, you can enter your AMP 52-character key here and it will generate the TOTP code for you.
If you failed to record the 52-character key, you will have to dig inside HA config files to retrieve it.

This worked. Thank you, @danielbrunt57

2858.bodyText:
Describe the bug
When the state of a sensor in a device integrated using AMP changes, it often takes 20 minutes or more to trigger automations. I have an automation set up to turn on a window fan and blow cold outside air into my room when the temperature in my room goes above 69 degrees. It often doesn't trigger until it is well over 70. Then when the cold air is blowing into my room, there is an automation set up to turn off the fan when the temperature gets down below 67. My teeth are chattering when the automation finally triggers and it's 62 degrees in my room!!
To Reproduce

Create an automation using a sensor on a device integrated through AMP.
Wait for the condition to trigger.
Wait even longer after the condition is satisfied before the command is executed.

Expected behavior

Create an automation using a sensor on a device integrated through AMP.
Wait for the condition to trigger.
Observe the command being immediately executed.

Screenshots
I've noticed that the sensor history (below) shows that the sensor is only updating once every 22 minutes or so.

System details


Home Assistant version:
Core 2025.2.5
Supervisor 2025.03.2
Operating System 14.2
Frontend 20250221.0


Is Amazon 2FA/2SV enabled <!---We will not debug login issues if unanswered---> (y/n): YES

firstcommentafterclosing.body:
Thank you so much for taking the time to reply. Looks like my best solution
is a separate temperature sensor.

I still use AMP for a lot of other stuff for which it works great. Thanks
again!!

 Amazon does not push any of that information. The updates only occur
 during polling so will never be or even near real time. And too frequent
 polling will be denied by Amazon. With a configured polling frequency of 60
 seconds and HTTP2PUSH connected (which is ONLY for echo device info like
 volume settings), the frequency is once every ten minutes as HTTP2PUSH
 always increases polling frequency by a factor of 10.

lastcommentbeforeclosing.body: Amazon does not push any of that information.  The updates only occur during polling so will never be or even near real time.  And too frequent polling will be denied by Amazon.  With a configured polling frequency of 60 seconds and HTTP2PUSH connected (which is ONLY for echo device info like volume settings), the frequency is once every ten minutes as HTTP2PUSH always increases polling frequency by a factor of 10.

firstcommentafterclosing.body: Remove Alexa Media Player, ensure /config/.storage/alexa_media*pickle does not exist, change your Amazon account password, configure your Amazon account with 2-Step Verification via Authenticator App, reinstall Alexa Media Player and provide the 52-character secret key from the Amazon 2SV authenticator app that you added to your account.

If you did not record it, the previously entered key will be in an HA database file in /config/.storage/ but the exact filename escapes me and I can't look at my HA since all my equipment is currently in storage as I'm in the middle of moving. The config entry contains "alexa_media" and I believe the key name is "otp_secret".

2862.bodyText:
IMPORTANT: Please search the issues, including closed issues, and the FAQ before opening a new issue. The template is mandatory; failure to use it will result in issue closure.
Describe the bug
The media_player entities remain in the playing state after playback is stopped.
Before version 5.5.0, the state would correctly switch back to idle after playback ended.
Now the state stays on playing, which causes the dashboard to always show an active media player — even though nothing is actually playing.
In the Alexa app, the media appears as “paused” and disappears after some time.
However, even after the playback display disappears in the Alexa app, the state in Home Assistant remains stuck on playing.
After reloading the alexa_media_player integration the playing status is removed.
To Reproduce

1.	Start media playback on an Echo device.
2.	Stop playback (e.g., by saying “Alexa, stop”).
3.	Observe the media_player entity in Home Assistant.

Expected behavior
The media_player entity should return to the idle state after playback is stopped, as it did before version 5.5.0.
Screenshots
–
System details

Home Assistant version: 2025.3.1
alexa_media version (from const.py or HA startup log): v5.6.0
alexapy version (from pip show alexapy in homeasssistant container or HA startup log): 1.29.5
Is Amazon 2FA/2SV enabled <!---We will not debug login issues if unanswered---> (y/n): y
Amazon Domain: amazon.de

Debug Logs (alexa_media & alexapy)
Please provide logs.

Fire TV devices and tablets show as normal, have performed many reauthentication's uninstalls, downgrades and reinstalls, every time all my devices come back, but the echo devices show as 'unavailable'

firstcommentafterclosing.body: You are likely getting alexapy errors in your log "Too many Requests" which come from Amazon, the reasons for which can be varied. One reason could be something in your Amazon account. In a similar scenario not too long ago, the user created a new account and it worked fine. Amazon never could find/fix the problem in his old account, not were they overly willing to since their app worked fine.

lastcommentafterclosing.body:
Thanks.
I resolved it by uninstalling the add-on in HACS, deleting the custom components folder, rebooting and then doing. A complete reinstall and reconfiguration.

2865.bodyText:
Hi Team,  First I love this integration when it works.  From time to time reauth breaks for whatever reason and I get super frustrated in trying to navigate the reconfigure effectively the manual reauth flow.
Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.
Example case:
I was testing out a new NodeRed integration that used Alexa v3 API for integration (just for reference https://docs.cb-net.co.uk/en/development/).  After linking it to my account via the alexa app and adding a fake light to see if the calling speaker id was being passed.  The alexa_media_player integration started having auth issues.  This may just be a coincidence.  I also notice that I get an OTP text from amazon.  This is normally how I know there is an auth problem with the Alexa Media Player integration.
What I did to try and resolve the issue.

I noticed there was a reconfiguration button for the Alexa Media Player integration.
I clicked on the reconfig button.  The HA Modal loaded with all the  FQDN urls, OTP key, etc just as I had set it up the first time.
It forced me to open the new tab with the url redirect link. (Note all of this is in FireFox)
The page loads with "Create a new account" which is annoying and the web page seems very oddly sized and styled.
I find the exiting account radio button enter my creds and complete the manual recaptcha.
The process completes and the redirect back to a page fails.  Checking the log it just says there was a problem with contacting the local internal IP of the computer I did this from.  Which is confusing as I would expect the redirect to go back to the HomeAssistant internal IP. Note: I have my own internal DNS server that when at home homeassistant public DNS resolves to internal IPs.  This is still not the IP for the failed redirect.
Even after the failure the reauth card doesn't go away.
Over the next 8 hours I get 4 more reauth cards.

Just to try to clear everything up I do a full homeassistant restart interrupting all services.  At time of writing I believe the integration to be in working order.
Describe the solution you'd like
Give the above use case.  Can you please update the existing documentation explain
1)What things cause a reauth flow to fail and the need for a reconfigure card to appear.
2)The expected process including screen shots for a reconfigure card when successfully performed.  Include the redirects, supported browsers, and when the card should disappear.
2.A)Give some known pitfalls of this flow from my use case or others.
3) Why am I getting OTP text from amazon when I have them in an OTP app?

I'm not asking for this to be exhaustive but to work as a starting point for end users who want to understand the complexities of the flow can build from and hopefully be less frustrated.
Additional context
Browser:  Firefox
Advanced Networking considerations:

Internal DNS services resolve public domains to internal IPs

2877.bodyText:
If you are being spammed by OTP's via SMS from Amazon, please try the following:
Remove your AMP integration config entry.
Remove 2SV from your Amazon account.
Decide on and set up an authenticator app to use when you log into Amazon if you're not already using one for other account logins.
Re-add 2SV to your Amazon account with 2SV OTP via Authenticator App as the primary method and add it to your authenticator app for login purposes. Also, click "can't scan the QR code" and record the 52-character key so you can use it in AMP or, add another authenticator app to your 2SV for AMP to use (refer to the AMP Wiki).
Re-add the AMP integration entering the app key into the config entry.
If this resolves your issue, add a thumbs up 👍
If not, add a thumbs down 👎.
Please do not post something like "Yes it worked" or "No, my problem still exists".  Questions about the steps are fine.
Thanks!!!

source.title: Failed to set up / Server got itself in trouble

firstcommentafterclosing.body: You did not enter the Chiave app 2FA integrata. It's the 52 character key from Amazon's 2SV Authenticator App method needed by AMP to self-generate an OTP everytime it needs to reaithenticate itself with Amazon. Click on Can't scan the QR code to reveal the 52-character key when you add an app to your 2SV. It must be the primary method and not a backup method. If you have a different primary method, you have to remove 2SV from your account and start over re-adding 2SV with Authenticator App as the primary (first) method.

firstcommentafterclosing.body: AMP was derived from more than just that script. A lot of it's concepts and methods came from work done by Apollon77's coding which is JS whereas AMP is Python so you'll not find any direct "code snippets" per-se.  Apollon77's current JS library works better than the current AMP but trying to rewrite that JS library in Python from scratch & get it work in the HA environment is a monumental task. You'd have to be proficient in developing in both languages to pull it off. We now have AI tools like ChatGPT to convert JS to Python but even with that there would still be a lot of work. And to try and adapt that JS library into AMP means you'd have to know all aspects of AMP pretty much inside out, as well as understanding all the aspects of alexa-remote2 JS code.

firstcommentafterclosing.body:
Reopening — my apologies if the logs weren't clear, but I’ve confirmed via debug logs that the login is in fact successful. The session is created, OTP is accepted (via Authenticator App), and cookies are retrieved successfully.
However, the device list returned is None, which leads to the 'NoneType' object is not subscriptable error.
I’ve followed the full configuration wiki, verified my devices exist in alexa.amazon.com, and even ran alexapy outside of HA to confirm the behavior.
It’s likely related to the behavior in #2890 where the login succeeds but Amazon’s API fails to return devices (possibly due to account rate limits, invalid tokens, or quarantined sessions post-reset).
Please let me know if there’s a way I can log or inspect the raw response from the /devices call or if there’s a specific proxy/device registration step I’m missing.
✅ Login does succeed — TOTP generated, session cookies are valid, and /auth/register responds 200 with access tokens and device registration.
❌ The issue is with the next step, where the integration attempts to fetch the device list — it fails with:
Error communicating with API: 'NoneType' object is not subscriptable
This suggests the /devices API call is returning an empty or malformed payload (possibly None), not that the login itself failed.
This matches symptoms in issue #2890. I’ve verified:

2FA is via authenticator app
Devices are listed at https://alexa.amazon.com
alexapy version is 1.29.5
AMP is installed via HACS with version 5.6.0
Home Assistant Core is 2025.3.4

home-assistant_alexa_media_2025-04-11T21-40-30.943Z.log

lastcommentafterclosing.body:
Try. v5.7.0 just released. Not sure if it will fix your issue or not...

I upgraded it within minutes of it's release, and bang, all my entities popped up.
Thank you for your dedication and help, I really, truly, appreciate your efforts.

lastcommentbeforeclosing.body:
For approximately 2 weeks before the integration broke, I was receiving daily Amazon 2FA SMS codes — sometimes multiple times per day — without any action on my part.

With Authenticator App as the primary and only method in Amazon 2SV, Amazon cannot issue OTP via SMS. It should be okay to set OTP via SMS as a backup method though. Then if you receive OTP via SMS, that would mean that both AMP's reauthentication via session token renew and credentials login via Auth App OTP has failed for some reason and Amazon has resorted to issuing an SMS OTP.

I’m confused with this one. I can add the authentication app successfully via the UI both in home assistant and on Amazon.

Originally posted by @TonyMontuna in #2893
IMPORTANT: Please search the issues, including closed issues, and the FAQ before opening a new issue. The template is mandatory; failure to use it will result in issue closure.
Please see above and referenced incorrectly closed issue

After reinstalling the Alexa Media Player integration and completing authentication successfully (including 2FA via the authenticator app), the integration completes without errors but no Alexa devices or notify services appear in Home Assistant. Debug logs confirm authentication is successful, but the device list retrieval fails, resulting in 'NoneType' object is not subscriptable.


Remove the Alexa Media Player integration from HA
Clear alexa-related files in .storage, including:

.storage/alexa
.storage/alexa_media.YOUR_EMAIL.pickle


Restart Home Assistant
Re-add the Alexa Media Player integration via Settings > Devices & Services
Log in using:

Amazon email and password
2FA using secret key from authenticator app (not SMS)
Code via https://www.amazon.com/code


Submit form and wait for devices to populate (nothing appears)

Expected behavior
The integration should populate all Alexa devices and associated media_player and notify.alexa_media_* entities after a successful login, as it did prior to the issue. Previously, 17 devices and over 100 entities were available.
Screenshots

Can provide sanitized screenshots of logs or UI if helpful.
System details

Home Assistant version: core-2025.3.4 (Home Assistant OS)
alexa_media version (from const.py or HA startup log): 5.6.0
alexapy version (from pip show alexapy in homeasssistant container or HA startup log): 1.29.5
Is Amazon 2SV via Authenticator App (not 2SV OTP via SMS) enabled? (y/n): y
Amazon Domain: amazon.com

Debug Logs (alexa_media & alexapy)
Highlights from the log (sanitized):
Creating TOTP for A********************E7A Generating OTP 863394 Using cookies to log in Testing whether logged in to alexa.amazon.com Successfully registered Anthony's Alexa Media Player device with Amazon New access token(353) received which expires at 2025-04-11 ... Error fetching alexa_media data: Error communicating with API: 'NoneType' object is not subscriptable
This indicates the login process is working as expected:

TOTP is accepted
Cookies and access token are retrieved
Amazon responds to session requests with HTTP 200 or 204
However, the /devices call either returns None or an empty payload

No media_player.alexa_* or notify.alexa_media_* entities appear in Home Assistant. No errors are shown in the frontend. Only the backend log shows the failed device fetch.
Additional context

I confirmed that all Alexa devices are still present and online at https://alexa.amazon.com.
I followed the wiki step-by-step, including:

2FA setup via Authenticator App
Secret key entry during integration
Use of local IP URL (http://192.168.x.x:8123) for proxy login
Leaving "include_devices" and "exclude_devices" fields empty


I attempted to test the login via alexapy in a standalone script, but Home Assistant OS lacks venv/pip support. I prepared to run the test in a Docker container as an alternative.
This behavior seems consistent with issue #2890 — successful login, no device list returned, and NoneType errors during update.


If there's a way to enable deeper logging for the device fetch step or manually test the /devices call, I'm happy to do so. I’d also be willing to re-run the login test in a Docker container to provide a sanitized raw payload from Amazon if helpful.
Thanks for all your work on this integration — happy to assist further!

2925.bodyText:
Home Assistant Core Version: 2025.6
Alexa Media Player Version: 5.7.2
Platform: Home Assistant OS
Browser/Client: Windows 10
Describe the bug:
After upgrading from Home Assistant 2024.7 to 2025.6, all of my Onelink Safe & Sound devices (WHA class) stopped responding to notify.alexa_media service calls. These devices had worked reliably for years prior. My Echo devices (e.g., Echo Dot, Echo Show) still work as expected.
Steps to reproduce the issue:

Upgrade from Home Assistant 2024.7 to 2025.6.
Send a notify.alexa_media message using type: tts or announce to a media_player entity for a Safe & Sound device.
Observe no output from the device, and no last_called or media activity.
View logs showing repeated “unavailable”/“unknown” states and occasional CancelledError exceptions.

What I tried:

Reloaded and reauthenticated Alexa Media Player.
Rebooted Home Assistant and devices.
Verified Onelink devices are controllable via Alexa app and routines.
Changed type to announce and split service calls by device family.
Monitored Developer Tools → States: device appears available, but last_called is always null and media_content_type stuck in standby.

Log samples:
homeassistant.helpers.service: Referenced entities media_player.royce_s_onelink_safe_sound are missing or not currently available
alexapy.helpers: CancelledError: alexaapi.get_customer_history_records...

Additional info:

All Onelink Safe & Sound devices cycle between unknown, unavailable, and briefly available.
Entity cards for timers/alarms/DND frequently go unavailable.
Echo devices do not exhibit this problem.
This issue may be related to a change in how WHA-class Alexa devices are handled in 2025.6.

lastcommentbeforeclosing.body: Somehow the identifier changed slightly for the onelink device.  When I upgraded no devices worked so I reinstalled the integration and apparently it changed the identifier for the onelink devices at that time and when the actions were ran the echo devices worked but not the onelink devices.  Sorry for the bother.

2927.bodyText:
While working on the alexa_media_player project, we identified a critical vulnerability in the pycares package, which provides Python bindings for the c-ares asynchronous DNS library (this dependency used by alexa_media_player). The issue, disclosed as a use-after-free vulnerability, arises when the Channel object is garbage collected while DNS queries are still pending, potentially leading to fatal crashes or undefined behavior.
CVE Link
CVE Report

2934.bodyText:
Describe the bug
After installing the Alexa Media Player integration via HACS and completing the login with 2FA, no Alexa devices or media_player entities appear in Home Assistant. The login is confirmed successful in the logs (Logged in as ...), but no entities are created or listed.
To Reproduce
Install Alexa Media Player via HACS
Go to Settings → Devices & Services → Add Integration
Choose Alexa Media Player, enter valid Amazon credentials (region: amazon.de)
Complete 2FA (via Authenticator App)
Observe successful login in logs
No Alexa devices or entities are shown in Home Assistant
Expected behavior
After successful authentication, I expected my Echo devices (Echo Dot, Echo Show) to appear in HA as media_player entities and notify.alexa_media_* services.
Screenshots

System details

Home Assistant version:
alexa_media version (from const.py or HA startup log):
alexapy version (from pip show alexapy in homeasssistant container or HA startup log):
Is Amazon 2SV via Authenticator App (not 2SV OTP via SMS) enabled? <!---We will not debug login issues if unanswered---> (y/n):
Amazon Domain:

System details
Home Assistant version: 2025.7.1
alexa_media version:
const.py.txt
alexapy version: Loaded alexapy==1.26.5
2FA (Authenticator App) enabled: Yes ✅
Amazon Domain: amazon.de
Debug Logs (alexa_media & alexapy)
✅ After login:
[custom_components.alexa_media] Logged in as alexa@example.de
[custom_components.alexa_media] Setup complete
❌ Earlier (before reauth & cleanup):
[custom_components.alexa_media] Error fetching alexa_media data: Error communicating with API: 'NoneType' object is not subscriptable
[alexapy.helpers] alexaapi.get_guard_details(...) ... TypeError: 'NoneType' object is not subscriptable
📁 I have deleted all alexa_media.* files from .storage, re-added the integration, completed 2FA login again, and restarted Home Assistant.
📌 Login works now — but no devices appear in HA.
Additional context
Devices are visible in my Amazon account (Echo Dot, Echo Show)
No errors appear after setup
I have verified debug logging and checked for filtered/disabled entities – no Alexa-related ones are present

source.title: Stopped working with Home Assistant update to core 2025.7.1
fix: Remove deprecated call to get_network_details
fix: remove deprecated call to get_network_details (#2943)

2941.bodyText:
Hi team,
My Alexa Media Player integration stopped working unexpectedly today. I'm running Home Assistant OS with Core version 2025.6.1.
Reproduce:
Installed Alexa Media Player via HACS
Went to Settings → Devices & Services → Add Integration
Selected Alexa Media Player
Entered valid Amazon credentials (region: amazon.com)
Completed 2FA using manual Authenticator 52 digits

App key
Observed a successful login in the logs
But: No Alexa devices or entities appear in Home Assistant
I’ve already:
Removed and re-added the integration
Restarted Home Assistant
Confirmed devices are visible in https://www.amazon.com/hz/mycd/digital-console/alldevices?pageType=devices&ref_=ya_d_l_manage_devices under the same account
Please advise on how to proceed or further troubleshoot this issue.

source.title: fix: Remove deprecated call to get_network_details
fix: remove deprecated call to get_network_details (#2943)

firstcommentafterclosing.body: Really appreciate your work, but this doesn't fix this issue! Still got same logs error, still got last alexa sensor "unavailable". I will provide any additional information if requested!

lastcommentafterclosing.body:
This fix addresses the issue where all functionality stopped when the get_network_details() call, which was previously available, is no longer available, and instead makes it function again by not making the get_network_details() call. However, because the data obtained using the get_network_details() call is no longer available, child devices of Alexa devices and the last Alexa sensor remain unavailable. This appears to be the current specification.
It is possible that functionality that was no longer available may be restored using other API calls, but this would require contributions from users.

IMPORTANT: Please search the issues, including closed issues, and the FAQ before opening a new issue. The template is mandatory; failure to use it will result in issue closure.
Describe the bug

I am unable to configure the Alexa Media Player. I have been using the Media Player for many months, but because of some issues, I deleted it and reinstalled. When I try to configure it, I receive "Invalid Authenticator App key for Amazon 2SV".
I have no problem logging in to Amazon.com directly using 2SV with the same email and authenticator app.
To Reproduce


Settings, Add integration
Choose Alexa Media Player
Fill in config
See error "Invalid Authenticator App key for Amazon 2SV"
Dazzle shows error "WARNING (MainThread) [alexapy.alexalogin] Error creating TOTP; ****** likely invalid"

Expected behavior

Configuration should be successful
Screenshots



System details

Home Assistant version: Core 2025.6.1
alexa_media version (from const.py or HA startup log): v5.7.3
alexapy version (from pip show alexapy in homeasssistant container or HA startup log):
Is Amazon [2SV via Authenticator App] (https://github.com/alandtse/alexa_media_player/wiki/Configuration#enable-two-factor-authentication-for-your-amazon-account) (not 2SV OTP via SMS) enabled? <!---We will not debug login issues if unanswered---> (y/n): y
Amazon Domain: amazon.com

Debug Logs (alexa_media & alexapy)
Please provide logs.
Additional context
Since I cannot configure, I don't think that I can enable debugging, but  Dazzle shows error "WARNING (MainThread) [alexapy.alexalogin] Error creating TOTP; ****** likely invalid"
